judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue but deeply unprofitable with a highly leveraged balance sheet.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimate by 16.3%; next earnings date 2026-05-07.
positives: Revenue growth 111.6% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Gross margin 69.4% — strong pricing power on services.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $138.90.
negatives: Operating margin -6.9% and Profit margin -25.6% — significant losses on operations.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-8.56B — burning cash at an alarming rate.
Debt/Equity 738.54 and Total debt $35.15B vs Total cash $2.27B — extremely high leverage.
Forward P/E -161.53 and Analyst growth est, current FY -136.5% — priced for extreme future growth that is not materializing.

news.paragraph: CoreWeave is reportedly building its enterprise business, particularly within financial services, while a recent film production highlighted significant AI compute costs. The company's focus on enterprise solutions suggests a strategic push for revenue growth, but the substantial AI compute expenditure in a creative project underscores the capital-intensive nature of its operations. Traders should monitor demand trends in its core markets against the backdrop of high operational costs.

headlines.title: This Cannes Film Cost $500,000 to Make. $400,000 Was AI Compute Costs.
CoreWeave Building Up Enterprise Business, Led By Financial Services
